rag_rules: Generate AGREE annex blocks inside component implementation scopes when the requirement item is an input_assumption
Use the 'assume' keyword for input_assumption items
Place assumptions in the annex agree block of the target component implementation
Use the visible identifier from the requirement item's evidence as the expression in the assume clause
Do not generate guarantees or other clause types for input_assumption items
generic_rules: Use the visible identifier from the requirement item's evidence as the expression in the assume clause
Place assumptions in the annex agree block of the target component implementation
Use the 'assume' keyword for input_assumption items
Do not generate guarantees or other clause types for input_assumption items
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use the syntax `assume "label": expression;` for input assumptions
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions in component type annexes unless they describe interface behavior
Do not generate guarantees or other clause types for input_assumption items
Do not use AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 1
